subject: Changes to accomodate to version of jetty
commit_message:
Changes to accomodate to version of jetty

With a newer version of jetty being used, a couple of methods have
been deprecated.  Modified the code to use the facilities appropriate
to the new jetty version.

Change-Id: Idcaef11836841480c24b1721c06ed642d3d4394a
Issue-ID: POLICY-2100
Signed-off-by: Jim Hahn <jrh3@att.com>

entries.message:
Patch Set 1: Verified+1

Build Successful 

https://jenkins.onap.org/job/policy-common-master-verify-java/686/ : SUCCESS

Logs: https://logs.onap.org/production/vex-yul-ecomp-jenkins-1/policy-common-master-verify-java/686

Patch Set 1: -Code-Review

Where did the jetty version number change go?   I don't see a dependency or dependency management section in parent or here that specifies the version, which would be an issue for deterministic builds.   Am I missing it somewhere?

Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1

while we get some clarity .. It seems to me that we are missing the dependencyManagement section in policy/parent for the jetty libraries, bringing unplanned jetty upgrades.

Patch Set 1:

> while we get some clarity .. It seems to me that we are missing the
 > dependencyManagement section in policy/parent for the jetty
 > libraries, bringing unplanned jetty upgrades.

It was changed in policy-endpoints pom, sometime in the past.  Whenever that change was made, eclipse started flagging these lines.

Patch Set 1:

oparent upgrade is where Jetty versions were defined. We upgraded policy/parent a couple of weeks ago.

Patch Set 1:

> Patch Set 1:
> 
> oparent upgrade is where Jetty versions were defined. We upgraded policy/parent a couple of weeks ago.

They were certainly planned - please review the El Alto scrums where I described the work I did for this. It was a security upgrade.
